product.name: Pathology Lab Report Manager
product.tagline: Digitise test orders, auto-generate PDF reports, and send results to patients via WhatsApp

sections.problem_statement: Small-to-mid-sized pathology labs in India currently rely on manual data entry and fragmented paper-based workflows that lead to high error rates and delayed patient communication. Existing LIMS solutions are often prohibitively expensive, overly complex, and lack native integration with high-frequency communication channels like WhatsApp. This product provides a lightweight, mobile-first interface to digitize test orders and automate report delivery, ensuring compliance with the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act while slashing administrative overhead.
target_users: Dr. Anjali Sharma, Pathologist & Lab Owner, Tier-2 city private lab, struggles with manual transcription errors in patient reports.
Rajesh Kumar, Lab Technician, Diagnostic Center, spends 3 hours daily manually emailing/printing reports instead of processing samples.
Sunita Devi, Frequent Patient, chronic diabetic, finds it difficult to track historical test trends across multiple paper reports.
user_stories: As a Lab Owner, I want to digitize patient test orders via a mobile form so that I can reduce data entry time by 50%.
As a Lab Technician, I want to auto-generate PDF reports from template fields so that I can eliminate manual document formatting errors.
As a Patient, I want to receive my lab reports directly on WhatsApp so that I can access my health data instantly without visiting the lab.
As a Lab Owner, I want to maintain a digital audit trail of all generated reports so that I remain compliant with NABH and DPDP Act regulations.
As a Lab Manager, I want to see a daily dashboard of processed tests so that I can forecast reagent inventory requirements.
mvp_features: Digital Order Entry: Mobile-friendly form to capture patient demographics, referring doctor, and test codes to initiate the workflow.
Smart PDF Engine: Automated report generation using pre-configured templates mapped to specific pathology tests (e.g., CBC, Lipid Profile).
WhatsApp API Integration: Direct pipeline to send password-protected PDF reports to patients immediately upon technician approval.
Referral Doctor Portal: Automated email/WhatsApp notification to the referring physician once the patient report is finalized.
Data Privacy Layer: Mandatory consent capture for data processing and end-to-end encryption to ensure compliance with DPDP Act standards.
Basic Analytics Dashboard: Real-time view of daily test volume, pending reports, and revenue summary.
future_features: AI-Powered OCR: Extracting data from old prescription slips to auto-fill order forms.
E-Prescription Integration: Direct API links with local clinics to receive digital test orders.
Longitudinal Health Tracking: Visual trend analysis for patients to track markers like HbA1c over time.
Inventory Management: Automated alerts for reagent stock levels based on test consumption rates.
success_metrics: Report Turnaround Time (TAT): Reduce from 24 hours to under 4 hours for 90% of reports within 3 months.
WhatsApp Delivery Success Rate: Achieve 98% delivery rate for all generated reports within the first 6 months.
Customer Acquisition: Onboard 50 independent labs in the first quarter via direct field sales and regional diagnostic associations.
Churn Rate: Maintain monthly churn below 5% for paying labs, signaling high product-market fit.
assumptions: Lab owners in Tier-2 cities are willing to move away from paper-based registers if the ROI is demonstrated within 30 days.
Patients prefer receiving reports via WhatsApp over traditional email or physical collection.
The cost of WhatsApp Business API messaging will remain within the sustainable margin for the subscription pricing model.
risks: Data Privacy Breach → Mitigation: Implement AES-256 encryption at rest and in transit, with mandatory periodic security audits.
Low Digital Literacy of Staff → Mitigation: Design an intuitive 'one-tap' UI and provide 24/7 vernacular support via video tutorials.
Regulatory Changes in Health Data → Mitigation: Build a flexible data architecture that allows for quick updates to consent and storage protocols.
sections.go_to_market: We will launch via direct B2B sales targeting independent pathology labs and diagnostic chains in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Indian cities. Our acquisition strategy centers on 'Free-Trial' pilots with regional diagnostic associations to build trust and social proof. The first 100 customers will be serviced through a high-touch onboarding playbook, ensuring they achieve a 20% reduction in TAT within the first month of usage.
sections.tech_stack_suggestion: The product will use a No-Code/Low-Code stack: FlutterFlow for the mobile interface, Xano as the backend/database, and Retool for the internal admin dashboard. Integration with the WhatsApp Business API will be handled via Twilio or Gupshup, utilizing a serverless architecture on AWS Lambda for PDF generation. This setup ensures scalability while minimizing initial development costs.
sections.monetisation: We will utilize a tiered SaaS subscription model: Basic at ₹1,999/month for small labs, Pro at ₹3,999/month for mid-sized centers, and Enterprise for large chains. The model is based on per-lab usage to ensure affordability for small businesses while scaling with their test volume. Expansion revenue will be driven by value-added services like automated patient follow-up reminders and digital marketing tools for labs.
